introSection: Space Mountain (Japanese: スペース・マウンテン) was a steel enclosed roller coaster formerly located at Tokyo Disneyland in Urayasu, Chiba, Japan. The ride closed in 2024 and a new Space Mountain is opening nearby in 2027.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The ride opened in 1983.

On 27 April 2022, The Oriental Land Company announced that Space Mountain would close in 2024 to make way for a complete reimagining of Tomorrowland done in a new, more futuristic style. The new Tomorrowland, including a new Space Mountain, is set to open in 2027.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Space Mountain was an enclosed roller coaster, similar to Disney's other Space Mountains. It was overhauled at certain times of the year. For example, during Halloween, the ride became Space Mountain: Ghost Galaxy.

fullText:
Space Mountain (Japanese: スペース・マウンテン) was a steel enclosed roller coaster formerly located at Tokyo Disneyland in Urayasu, Chiba, Japan. The ride closed in 2024 and a new Space Mountain is opening nearby in 2027.

The ride opened in 1983.

On 27 April 2022, The Oriental Land Company announced that Space Mountain would close in 2024 to make way for a complete reimagining of Tomorrowland done in a new, more futuristic style. The new Tomorrowland, including a new Space Mountain, is set to open in 2027.

Space Mountain was an enclosed roller coaster, similar to Disney's other Space Mountains. It was overhauled at certain times of the year. For example, during Halloween, the ride became Space Mountain: Ghost Galaxy.

Black track and supports.
